title: Release Gates, Capacity, Compliance, Migration, Rollback, and Defense · Program Forensics
summary: A preserved crawl trace contains one plausible authorization, URL, robots, HTTP, decoding, parser, selector, frontier, concurrency, metadata, accessibility, privacy, export, browser, or release defect; locate the first failed invariant before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in production crawler evidence and recovery defense and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, cau…
objectives: Reconcile authorization, scope, robots, HTTP, URL, parse, extraction, crawl graph, metadata, accessibility, output, and changed-case evidence.
Measure pages, resources, bytes, parse time, memory, queue, concurrency, browser load, output, cancellation, and recovery at representative scale.
Obtain accountable owner, privacy, security, accessibility, data, legal-review, operations, and target-site approvals with expiry and revocation paths.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Production Defense
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: scraper-production-defense-17l99w" that uses case 5610 to defend end-to-end correctness, capacity, accountable approvals, migration, owner pause, rollback, recovery, and residual risk. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, contact sites, import or claim native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, or Playwright behavior, read or write host files, use credentials, launch browsers, schedule host jobs, or claim live accessibility, privacy, legal, load, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, the recorded stable package versions, fixed and malformed fixtures, fake transports, controlled local servers, authorized disposable target sites, representative browser profiles, privacy, security, accessibility and legal review, load, migration, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Authorized crawling and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Production Defense.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Production Defense into observable performance evidence. For Authorized crawling and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Production Defense, keep Production Defense connected to executable Python authorization, URL, response, parse, provenance, frontier, report, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: scraper-production-defense-17l99w
# Competency: scraper-production-defense.
# Case 5610: a multilingual public-service site review.
# Operating constraint: personal data must remain minimized and redacted.
# Changed case: change a robots, canonical, indexing, structured-data, or accessibility signal and retain the conflict.
def evidence_scraper_production_defense_17l99w(release_gates: int = 16, passed_gates: int = 16, rehearsed_failures: int = 8, open_critical_findings: int = 0, owner_pause_seconds: int = 20) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_17l99w = "17l99w-w99l71-5610"
    assert evidence_variant_17l99w
    evidence_axes_17l99w = (
        "authorization17l99w",
        "url5610",
        "responsew99l71",
        "tree17l99w5610",
        "frontier561017l99w",
        "reportw99l715610",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_17l99w)) == 6
    if release_gates < 12 or passed_gates < 0:
        return False, passed_gates, "release-depth"
    if passed_gates != release_gates:
        return False, passed_gates, "failed-release-gate"
    if rehearsed_failures < 6 or open_critical_findings > 0:
        return False, open_critical_findings, "recovery-gap"
    if owner_pause_seconds < 0 or owner_pause_seconds > 60:
        return False, owner_pause_seconds, "owner-stop-budget"
    assert passed_gates == release_gates and open_critical_findings == 0
    return True, passed_gates, "defensible-crawler-production"
